title: galium commune subvar. hirsutum
intro: Galium commune subvar. hirsutum, also known as beard trefoil, is a subvariety of the common galium plant, a perennial herb native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Galium commune subvar. hirsutum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, which are made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent to release its medicinal compounds.
